title: Fire vs Water
context: Nature, symbolism, change, persistence, and force
surface_question: Which is more powerful, fire or water?
a_root: Rapid physical transformation through energy release.
b_root: Adaptive molecular flow that carries, dissolves, nourishes, erodes, and reshapes.
a_causal_chain: fuel + oxygen + heat -> combustion -> energy release -> rapid transformation -> extinction without fuel
b_causal_chain: cohesion + gravity + pressure -> flow -> carrying/dissolving/erosion -> slow transformation
hidden_difference: Fire transforms by intensity. Water transforms by persistence.
a_failure_mode: Fire fails without fuel, oxygen, heat, or containment.
b_failure_mode: Water loses directed force without boundary, gradient, pressure, or time.
conditional_verdict: Fire wins when fast transformation is needed. Water wins when deep persistent transformation is needed.
final_line: Fire transforms by intensity. Water transforms by persistence.
